---
title: "News"
---

## 2026

**March 2026** — AI in Cancer Workshop: Advancing Precision Medicine through Interdisciplinary Innovation  
Yale School of Medicine hosted a workshop on AI applications in cancer care and precision medicine.  
[Yale profile news →](https://medicine.yale.edu/profile/guannan-gong/)

**January 2026** — Clinical Trial Patient Matching published in *JCO Clinical Cancer Informatics*  
Our CTPM system for semiautomated cancer trial prescreening was published, reporting validation across 29 oncology trials.  
[Read the paper →](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41512229/)

---

## 2025

**December 2025** — Research presented at San Antonio Breast Cancer Symposium  
Yale Cancer Center research advances, including informatics work relevant to trial matching, were presented at SABCS.  
[Yale Cancer Center →](https://www.yalecancercenter.org/)

**July 2025** — Blavatnik Accelerator Award  
Dr. Gong's translational biotech project was selected for the 2025 Blavatnik Accelerator Awards through Yale Ventures.  
[Yale Ventures announcement →](https://ventures.yale.edu/)

**May 2025** — Eligibility criteria NLP framework protocol published  
Our JMIR Research Protocols paper describing the LLM-based eligibility criteria visualizer was published.  
[Read the protocol →](https://www.researchprotocols.org/2026/1/e86425)

---

## 2024

**November 2024** — Yale Cancer Center Catchment Area Research Award  
Awarded for research advancing clinical trial access in the Yale Cancer Center catchment area.

**June 2024** — Yale New Haven Health Innovation Award  
CtrlTrial's Clinical Trial Patient Matching system received the YNHH Innovation Award.  
[YNHH announcement →](https://www.ynhhs.org/news/yale-new-haven-health-and-yale-university-celebrate-innovation-awards)

---

## 2022

**October 2022** — Yale Cancer Center T-TARE Award  
Supported development and validation of the OMOP-based clinical trial patient matching pipeline.

---

## 2020

**May 2020** — Startup Yale Rothberg Catalyzer Award  
Recognized early work on AI-driven clinical trial recruitment through Yale Tsai CITY.
